_meta.purpose: Durable structured dataset of primary-source SERFF rate filings — the analytical superset behind rate_changes.json. Feeds future charts/tools (liability-vs-physical-damage splits, rate trajectory, book-size weighting). rate_changes.json remains the tracker DISPLAY feed; this file is the RAW reference, joined by tracking #.

schema.indicated_pct: signed actuarially-indicated change (what they asked/justified) | null
schema.prior_revision_pct: signed % of the carrier's previous revision (trajectory) | null
schema.written_premium: total book $ for the program | null
schema.coverage_changes: per-coverage % {BI,PD,UM,MED,COMP,COLL,...} where the filing/PC-form exposed it | null (jacket-only filings don't break it out)
schema.premium_as_of: valuation date of written_premium/affected (PC form Part 2 '@ MM/DD/YYYY' header) — REQUIRED for market-share-over-time | null if not captured
schema.market_share_sc: carrier's stated % share of the SC auto market (SC PC form field) | null; SC-specific, other states' forms may differ

_meta.coverage_note: Per-coverage splits come from the PC form 'Part 1 / Requested Percent Changes by Type of Coverage' or a Rate Level Effect exhibit. Jacket-only zips (and image-based exhibits) don't expose them, so coverage_changes is null there. Capture Part 1 going forward when text-available — it powers the 'is your increase liability- or repair-driven' angle.
_meta.refind: Re-find any filing by searching its `tracking` number in SERFF Filing Access for that state (SERFF Tracking Number field) — works for '-G' numbers too, unlike the filingId deep link. We do NOT store individual attachment filenames; re-download the filing zip if a specific document (PC form, exhibit) is needed.

pulls.note: June only — still owes Oct 2025-May 2026 backfill
Selective (SELC-134783974, increase, prior +30%) deferred — exact % unread. Big finding: State Farm -8.1% on 1.2M PH.

Top-5 completion pull: State Farm, GEICO, USAA + Farmers/Nationwide/Farm Bureau. Material: SF Mutual -3.0% (2.07M PH, primary-sources the newsroom cut), GEICO +4.6% (176K PH), Farmers Group +5.0%. Flat: USAA full-limits, Farmers FLEX, Nationwide, GFBR main book. USAA min-limits +9.9% (segment). GA top-5 now all SERFF-sourced.

Top-5 completion: Progressive, GEICO, USAA all held SC FLAT (symbol/0% filings only) -> clears gate at 5/5, zero new movers. Nationwide 0% (bonus). Travelers pulled but recent SC activity is symbol-only (Quantum 1.0/2.0 Set U/Original symbols); last real Travelers rate revision eff 12/30/2025 NOT pulled. SC story: big shoppers' carriers holding flat while State Farm/Allstate cut.

TX is TDI open-data (data.texas.gov), NOT SERFF click-through — pulled entirely via Socrata API, joined percent_change (ittv-5xew) to auto index (iubg-btfs) on serff_id. No PH/premium in the data. TX broadly SOFTENING: State Farm -3%, Progressive -2.5%, Germania -7.9% (repeat cutter), Mid-Century -10.1%, Farmers -5.2%; USAA/Liberty/Safeco/Nationwide/Travelers flat; only GEICO +2% and Allstate (+2..+10% in 2026 after -11.4% in 2025) raising. Texas Farm Bureau: NO in-window auto filing.
pulls.roster_audit: Tool roster = 14 nationals + 3 TX regionals (Texas Farm Bureau, Germania, Mercury). Covered/verified all EXCEPT: The Hartford (no clean filing - AARP/fronting), Kemper, Mercury, Texas Farm Bureau (TX #1) = NO in-window auto filing. Root +1.2% + 5 flat nationals added in completeness pass.

_meta.count_note: NV PC forms report 'Number of Insured Vehicles'; GA jackets report 'Number of Policy Holders Affected' — different denominators (~1.5 vehicles/policy). written_premium/count therefore yields $/vehicle for NV, $/policy for GA. Record the exact basis; don't mix them in a per-unit average.

presentation_uses._intent: Fields captured not just for the model but to make the customer-facing rate presentation more credible/useful. Keep capturing these; they unlock the angles below.
presentation_uses.coverage_changes: "Your increase is liability-driven, not repair-cost" — split BI/UM vs COMP/COLL so a shopper sees WHY.
presentation_uses.indicated_pct vs overall_pct: "The carrier asked the state for +24%; the regulator approved -10%" — regulator-as-protection narrative.
presentation_uses.prior_revision_pct: Rate trajectory — "this carrier has raised twice this cycle (+7% then +5.5%)" vs a one-off.
presentation_uses.effective_new vs effective_renewal: "You won't see this until your renewal on <date> — shop before then" — the tracker's signature timing angle.
presentation_uses.written_premium + affected: Scale/credibility — "affects 109,120 drivers / a $404M book," and enables book-size-weighted charts.
presentation_uses.disposition_date + premium_as_of: Freshness/as-of recency — lets us say how current each figure is.
